channel.title: The Friendship Practice — Guides
channel.description: Practical guides on friendship by Carole Stromboni.

item.title: Why male friendships fade after 30
item.description: Male friendships don't end in a fight. They fade through distance, cancelled plans, and the slow disappearance of shared contexts. Here's what the research shows.

item.title: How men can rebuild friendships without making it awkward
item.description: You don't need a deep conversation or a vulnerability retreat. You need a recurring activity, one invitation, and a follow-up. Here's how to rebuild male friendship practically.

item.title: Friendship quotes: eight counter-intuitive observations from a friendship coach
item.description: Eight counter-intuitive observations on adult friendship from Carole Stromboni, friendship coach. On following up, loneliness, consistency, and what actually builds closeness — not inspiration, but clarity.

item.title: How to make friends as a new parent
item.description: New parenthood is one of the loneliest social transitions in adult life. A practical guide to meeting other parents, following up, and building friendships that survive the chaos of early parenthood.

item.title: How to keep work friendships after going remote
item.description: Work friendships often fade when you go remote, not because the connection disappears but because the structure that maintained it does. A practical guide to keeping those friendships alive.

item.title: How to make new friends after a friendship ends
item.description: When an important friendship ends or fades, rebuilding your social life can feel harder than starting from scratch. A practical guide to making new friends after losing a close one.

item.title: How to follow up after meeting someone
item.description: The follow-up after meeting someone new is where most adult friendships either begin or die. A practical guide to what to say, when to say it, and how to suggest a next step.

item.title: Why do friendships fade?
item.description: Most adult friendships do not end in conflict. They fade quietly, through no single decision and no dramatic break. Here is why it happens and what you can do about it.

item.title: How to turn acquaintances into friends
item.description: The gap between knowing someone and being friends with them is one of the most frustrating in adult social life. Here is how to cross it with specific, practical steps.

item.title: How to make friends as an expat
item.description: Making friends as an expat adds extra layers to an already difficult adult challenge: a new country, a different culture, and no existing social network. A practical guide to building real connection abroad.

item.title: How to know if someone wants to be your friend
item.description: Reading the signals of mutual interest is one of the hardest parts of adult friendship. A practical guide to recognizing when a connection has real potential.

item.title: What is friendship coaching?
item.description: Friendship coaching is practical support for adults who want to make new friends, reconnect with old ones, or deepen current friendships. Here is what it is, what it is not, and who it is for.

item.title: How to Stay Friends With Someone Who Just Had a Baby
item.description: Friendships often fade after one person has a child. Here is how to adjust your expectations, lower the bar for contact, and keep the connection real without overwhelming a new parent.

item.title: What to Do When a Friendship Feels One-Sided
item.description: Feeling like you are the only one putting effort into a friendship is exhausting. Here is how to recognize the pattern, understand what is driving it, and decide what to do.

item.title: How to Make Friends When You Work From Home
item.description: Remote work removes the easiest structure for repeated contact in adult life. Here is how to build a real social life deliberately when your home is also your office.

item.title: How to introduce friends to each other
item.description: A practical guide to introducing two friends who do not know each other yet, when to do it, how to ask both people first, and why it strengthens your whole social network.

item.title: When a friendship ends
item.description: How to understand, navigate, and sometimes consciously close a friendship, whether it fades, falls apart, or simply runs its course.

item.title: How to meet people in a new city
item.description: A practical guide to meeting people in a new city without relying on luck, with concrete ways to create repetition and turn early contact into connection.

item.title: How to be a better friend
item.description: A practical guide to being a better friend through attention, reliability, invitations, follow-through, and honest care in ordinary life.

item.title: How to make friends after 30
item.description: A practical guide to making friends after 30, with clear ways to create repetition, follow up, and build connection without pretending adult life is simple.

item.title: How to make friends as an adult
item.description: A practical guide to making friends as an adult, with specific steps for meeting people, following up, and turning early connection into real friendship.

item.title: How to reconnect with old friends
item.description: A practical guide to reconnecting with old friends without overexplaining the distance or waiting for the perfect message.

item.title: Why making friends as an adult is hard
item.description: Why adult friendship can feel so difficult, and what changes when you stop treating friendship as luck and start treating it as practice.

item.title: How to keep friends as an adult
item.description: A practical guide to keeping adult friendships alive through repetition, follow-through, invitations, and small acts of consistency.
